title: Research Brief
description: Structured web research findings that ground a video in real data, trends, and audience insights. Produced by the Research Director before any creative work begins.

topic.description: The core topic as stated by the user

research_date.description: When the research was conducted — freshness matters

landscape.description: What already exists in the content landscape

engagement_signal.description: View count, upvotes, comments — whatever is available

existing_content.description: Top existing content on this topic — at least 3 entries

saturated_angles.description: Angles that have been done to death — avoid these

underserved_gaps.description: Angles or questions that existing content does NOT cover well

trending.description: What is happening RIGHT NOW around this topic

relevance.description: Why this matters for our video

sentiment.description: What people feel about this — excitement, frustration, debate, confusion

key_quotes.description: Direct quotes that capture the community's voice

data_points.description: Verified facts and statistics. source_url deliberately kept required: it is the anti-fabrication check that a claim traces back to a real, checkable source. A real artifact observed asserting a claim with only a generic source_name (no URL at all) looks like an unverifiable claim, not an overly strict schema — see STEP 1 measurement notes.

claim.description: The specific fact, statistic, or data point

credibility.description: How reliable is this data point?

surprise_factor.description: How unexpected is this for the target audience?

usable_as.description: How this could be used: hook, stat_card, script_anchor, closing_punch

common_questions.description: Real questions people ask about this topic — sourced from forums, PAA, Quora

knowledge_level.description: What the target audience likely already knows vs. what will be new

pain_points.description: Frustrations, confusions, or complaints related to this topic

position.description: Their stance or notable quote on this topic

contrarian.description: Does this challenge the mainstream view?

expert_voices.description: Named experts and their takes — useful for authority and debate framing

type.description: What kind of angle is this?

why_now.description: Why this angle is compelling right now — cite research findings

grounded_in.description: Which data_points or audience_insights support this angle?

angles_discovered.description: Angle candidates discovered through research — raw material for the Proposal Director. additionalProperties intentionally open — real runs routinely attach extra creative-direction fields (audio_direction, motion_commitment, visual_direction, camera_approach, color_palette, editing_rhythm, risk, cinematic_references) that are legitimate detail, not typos.

what_works.description: Why this visual approach is effective

visual_references.description: How the best existing content visualizes this topic

used_for.description: Which section of the brief this source supports

sources.description: All sources used — at least 5. Full bibliography. used_for demoted from required and additionalProperties opened: real runs commonly describe a source's role via 'relevance'/'accessed_date' instead of 'used_for'. url+title (the actual verifiable citation) stay required.

research_summary.description: One paragraph: the single most important insight from this research and why it matters for the video
